acceptance_targets: [ch15-craft-001] Pass if the revised text at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:61, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:189, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:499 contains no chapter-number language and each beat reads as lived memory inside Mina's world without any meta awareness of the manuscript.

[ch15-prose-001] Pass if the revised beats anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:147, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:163, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:267, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:305, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:343, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:389, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:485, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:507, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:519 no longer stack the same corrective formula throughout the chapter: no single scene should rely on that template more than twice, and at least three of the currently flagged beats should instead land through concrete detail unique to that moment.

[FBR-007] Pass if snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:6905-6913 shows Rhea under pressure in a way that reads as hers rather than as generic pause language. Fail if the reaction still routes through a stock stillness beat instead of one of her established stress behaviors.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised text at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:61, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:189, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:499 contains no chapter-number language and each beat reads as lived memory inside Mina's world without any meta awareness of the manuscript.
findings.problem: The close-third narration directly names "chapter thirteen," "chapter fourteen," and "chapter nine." That is process leakage: manuscript scaffolding appears inside scene consciousness, breaks immersion, and violates the constitution's no-process-leak rule.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace the scaffold references at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:61, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:189, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:499 with diegetic memory cues. Name the earlier annex confrontation, paddleboard survey intimacy, or shared eel sighting by event, sensation, or consequence as Mina would actually remember them, not by chapter number.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised beats anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:147, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:163, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:267, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:305, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:343, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:389, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:485, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:507, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:519 no longer stack the same corrective formula throughout the chapter: no single scene should rely on that template more than twice, and at least three of the currently flagged beats should instead land through concrete detail unique to that moment.
findings.problem: The chapter overuses the same corrective-emphasis sentence shapes: "Not X. Y.", "That was X.", and "There it was." The first few hits sharpen pressure, but by the dock argument, casualty sequence, and eel coda the repetition reads as an autopilot cadence and flattens distinct beats into one reusable authorial rhythm.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:147, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:163, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:267, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:305, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:343, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:389, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:485, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:507, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:519, keep only the one or two strongest corrective fragments. Rebuild the other beats with fresh image, bodily sensation, or action so each scene lands through its own texture rather than the same negative-correction template.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:6905-6913 shows Rhea under pressure in a way that reads as hers rather than as generic pause language. Fail if the reaction still routes through a stock stillness beat instead of one of her established stress behaviors.
findings.problem: Full-book recurring-construction finding: the default-stillness shorthand appears in five chapters, and this use should be replaced. At the exact point where county pragmatism collides with rookery science, Rhea's 'went still' cue is less precise than her style-bible stress tell, which should sharpen rather than genericize her pressure response.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:6905-6913, REPLACE the stillness shorthand with a Rhea-specific reaction: hyper-exact wording, a clipped scientific/legal correction, an abrupt controlled silence, or another stress tell grounded in her profile. Do not use 'went still' or 'went flat.'

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the book's uncomfortable overlaps: desire entangled with access, wonder contaminated by biotech history, class need shaping erotic choices, and ecological harm rendered at creature scale. When a scene sits on the edge between restraint and retreat, keep the pressure visible through bodies, residue, changed behavior, or social cost. If an exact detail would make the material more embarrassing, more sensual, or more morally compromising, that is usually the detail to keep.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If sex, ecological damage, class shame, or moral compromise are softened into tasteful fog at the moment they should bite, the scene has failed and must be rewritten at higher specificity.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High - contractions are the norm in spoken dialogue; uncontracted forms should signal deliberate emphasis, institutional performance, or a character-specific formality shift.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate compression and dilation aggressively: single-scene nocturnes, comic paperwork chapters, quiet survey chapters, domestic weather chapters, confrontational set pieces, and aftermath chapters should sit beside each other without repeating the same rhythm two chapters in a row.
prose_style_profile.diction: Concrete, coastal, and exact without brochure prettiness; scientific and bureaucratic terms may appear when the characters would think or speak through them, but they should stay embodied by consequence.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third centered on Mina Cordero, with free-indirect access tight enough to track bodily desire, shame, and observational obsession without collapsing into confessional summary
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Supple sentence music that can hold humidity, rot, and erotic charge, then cut hard into blunt compression at moments of humiliation, procedural friction, or ecological damage.
